docs.text: Carbone | Italian-American | Greenwich Village | theatrical, red-sauce, celebrity-spot | date-night special-occasion scene tourist-magnet | The Major Food Group's Italian-American time machine. Tableside Caesars, lobster fra diavolo, a veal parm the size of a hubcap. Reservations drop 30 days out at 10am ET on Resy and evaporate in seconds. A reservation here is social currency in NYC.

Don Angie | Italian | West Village | intimate, mirrored, romantic | date-night instagram italian | Angie Rito and Scott Tacinelli's tiny West Village Italian, with a mirrored ceiling and the lasagna pinwheel that broke Instagram. Two-tops are the hardest get; group tables open more often. Repeat customers swear by the chrysanthemum salad.

Tatiana by Kwame Onwuachi | American / Caribbean / Afro-diasporic | Lincoln Square | lively, modern, music-driven | date-night special-occasion pre-theater of-the-moment | Kwame Onwuachi's love letter to the Bronx, perched at Lincoln Center. The NYT's 2023 Restaurant of the Year. Curry goat patties, oxtail dumplings, braised short rib pastrami. Big tables go faster than two-tops because of the pre-show theater crowd.

4 Charles Prime Rib | Steakhouse | West Village | clubby, dim, old-school | date-night business-dinner steakhouse | Brendan Sodikoff's clubby West Village steakhouse. The signature prime rib, the 4 Charles burger at the bar, and a wine list that rewards spending. Books out 30 days in advance the second the calendar opens. Late slots (9:30+) and bar seating are the realistic gets.

The Polo Bar | American | Upper East Side | Ralph Lauren clubhouse, equestrian, see-and-be-seen | business power-lunch scene uptown | Ralph Lauren's flagship restaurant: equestrian leather, brass tack lighting, a power-lunch crowd in cashmere. Famously hard to book without a connection. Lunch is slightly easier than dinner. Dress code enforced.

Rao's | Italian-American | East Harlem | owned-tables, mafia-lore, time-warp | bucket-list italian old-school | Tables are owned by regulars, on the same night each year. New reservations are functionally impossible — but cancellations do occur. The Sinatra-era room, the lemon chicken, the meatballs. A bucket-list restaurant for serious eaters.

Le Bernardin | French / Seafood | Midtown West | hushed, refined, fine-dining | fine-dining special-occasion michelin | Eric Ripert's three-Michelin-starred temple to seafood. A four-decade Times four-star. Refined, hushed, with service that anticipates needs. Lunch is a comparative bargain. Books 30 days in advance; window-table requests rarely honored.

Eleven Madison Park | Plant-based fine dining | Flatiron | ceremonial, plant-based, NoMa-influenced | fine-dining vegetarian special-occasion | Daniel Humm's reinvented plant-based tasting menu in the soaring Madison Square Park dining room. Three Michelin stars, often debated, never ignored. Bar room is the easier in-road and serves a more flexible menu.

Sushi Nakazawa | Sushi / Omakase | West Village | intimate, omakase, Jiro-protégé | omakase date-night fine-dining | Daisuke Nakazawa, of Jiro Dreams of Sushi, runs the bar in a tight West Village space. The omakase is the order. Counter seats are the experience; tables are not. Two-tops at the counter are the hardest get.

I Sodi | Tuscan Italian | West Village | candlelit, regional Italian, romantic | date-night italian romantic | Rita Sodi's candlelit Tuscan jewel box, expanded to a slightly larger room in 2022 but still nearly unbookable. The 12-layer lasagna and the negroni on tap. Couples plan trips around it.

Rezdôra | Emilia-Romagna Italian | Flatiron | fresh-pasta-focused, energetic, regional | pasta italian of-the-moment | Stefano Secchi's Emilia-Romagna pasta workshop. The Tour of Emilia tasting (seven pastas) is the move. Resy drops are 28 days out, 12pm ET. A perennial Top 10 list pick.

Lucali | Pizza | Carroll Gardens | candlelit, BYOB, no-reservations-but | pizza byob date-night walk-in | Mark Iacono's candlelit Carroll Gardens pizzeria. Walk-in only, with a list system that requires arriving when the doors open at 5pm. The thin-crust pie is the ur-Brooklyn-pie. BYOB.

L'Artusi | Italian | West Village | convivial, modern Italian, two-floor | italian group consistent | The West Village's reliable hit: roasted broccoli with chili and pecorino, ricotta gnocchi, garganelli with bolognese. Two floors absorb the demand but prime times still book a month out.

Lilia | Italian | Williamsburg | airy, pasta-driven, Missy Robbins | pasta italian brooklyn | Missy Robbins' converted-auto-body-shop pasta haven. The mafaldine with pink peppercorn, sheep's milk cheese ravioli. Drops 30 days out; Williamsburg's hardest table.

Misi | Italian | Williamsburg | industrial-chic, pasta + vegetables | pasta italian brooklyn | Missy Robbins' second act: a glass-walled pasta theater on the Williamsburg waterfront. Vegetable-forward antipasti, simple-looking pastas with deep technique. Bar seats are the most flexible option.

Atomix | Modern Korean | NoMad | tasting-menu counter, design-forward, two Michelin stars | tasting-menu korean michelin | Junghyun Park's two-Michelin-starred Korean tasting menu in a hidden NoMad townhouse. 14 courses, course cards designed by his wife Ellia. Books out 60 days in advance. The hardest counter in NYC.

Cote | Korean steakhouse | Flatiron | high-energy, dry-aged beef, Michelin-starred KBBQ | korean steakhouse group | The Michelin-starred Korean steakhouse blueprint: dry-aged USDA Prime, banchan that keep coming, soju cocktails. The Butcher's Feast is the classic order. Walk-in bar seats are realistic; reservations are not.

Estela | Mediterranean / wine bar | NoHo | wine-bar-but-elevated, upstairs hideaway | wine-bar date-night mediterranean | Ignacio Mattos's upstairs Houston Street hideaway: ricotta dumplings in burnt butter, beef tartare with sunchokes, the lamb-ribs-with-honey that everyone orders. The bar serves the full menu and is the path of least resistance.

Balthazar | French brasserie | SoHo | brass-and-mirrors, Parisian-fantasy | brasserie brunch group tourist | Keith McNally's enduring SoHo brasserie. The brunch is a tourist tradition; the late-night menu is for industry. Big tables open the most; weekend brunch the hardest.

Pastis | French brasserie | Meatpacking | Meatpacking-revival, see-and-be-seen | brasserie scene brunch | McNally's Meatpacking comeback. Steak frites, oysters, the scene. Brunch is mob-deep; weeknight dinners around 6:30 are the underrated slot.

Frenchette | French | Tribeca | natural-wine, modern-bistro, two-Michelin | french natural-wine michelin | Riad Nasr and Lee Hanson's reborn Tribeca bistro: rotisserie duck for two, natural wine, a chef-y crowd. The bar serves the full menu. Two Michelin stars as of 2024.

The Modern | American / Contemporary | Midtown / MoMA | fine-dining, museum-adjacent, two-Michelin | fine-dining business-lunch michelin | Two-Michelin-starred Danny Meyer flagship overlooking the MoMA sculpture garden. Pre-museum lunch is the move. The bar room is more flexible; the dining room is for occasions.

Per Se | French / American | Columbus Circle | ceremonial, three-Michelin, fine-dining | fine-dining michelin special-occasion | Thomas Keller's New York French Laundry. Three Michelin stars, $390+ tasting menu, salmon cornets and oysters and pearls. Dinner is the special occasion; salon menu in the lounge is the discovery hack.

Daniel | French | Upper East Side | ceremonial, two-Michelin, classic-haute | fine-dining business michelin | Daniel Boulud's UES temple. Two Michelin stars, tasting menus, a bar/lounge that punches above its weight. A good business-anniversary spot.

Torrisi | Italian-American | NoLita | Major Food Group, plush, neighborhood-grand | italian-american scene date-night | MFG's Puck Building flagship, a love letter to NY-Italian dining. Sticky ribs, scampi, the Sunday gravy. Drops with Carbone-level intensity.

Kjun | Korean-Cajun fine dining | Lower East Side | intimate, 12-seat counter, tasting menu | tasting-menu korean of-the-moment | Jae Lee's 12-seat counter, where Korean staples meet Louisiana technique. One Michelin star. Blackened gulf shrimp with kimchi. Booked the second tickets drop on Tock.

Via Carota | Italian / Tuscan | West Village | airy, no-reservations, walk-in | italian no-reservations walk-in | The Sodi/Williams sister-restaurant to I Sodi. As of 2023, takes reservations on Resy at 9am, 14 days out. The svizzerina (Tuscan-style burger) and insalata verde are the move.

Saint Julivert Fisherie | Seafood | Cobble Hill | tiny, seafood-focused, husband-wife | seafood brooklyn neighborhood-gem | Alex Raij and Eder Montero's Cobble Hill seafood snug. Mexican prawn aguachile, a singular fideos with squid. Tiny room — the demand is mostly local.

Lodi | Italian / Café | Rockefeller Center | all-day-café, Ignacio Mattos, Milanese | cafe italian business-lunch | Ignacio Mattos's Milan-inspired all-day café in Rockefeller Center. Aperitivo hour with cicchetti is the underrated way in. Lunch crowds skew midtown professional.

Shukette | Israeli / Middle Eastern | Chelsea | loud, hummus-forward, Ayesha Nurdjaja | middle-eastern group lively | Ayesha Nurdjaja's Chelsea hummus temple. The kataifi-wrapped halloumi and the laffa with everything. Group-friendly, prime-time is the squeeze.
